%% -- Article metainformation (author, title, ...) -----------------------------

%% TODO: Update with actual author information
\author{First Author\\University of Somewhere}
\Plainauthor{First Author}

%% Title
\title{Chat-First Data Analytics}
\Plaintitle{prompt2analytics: A Rust Toolkit for Econometrics and Data Analytics}
\Shorttitle{\pkg{prompt2analytics}: Econometrics in \proglang{Rust}}

%% Abstract
\Abstract{
  This paper presents \pkg{prompt2analytics}, a software toolkit that applies
  established tool-augmented language model techniques to econometric analysis.
  Natural language conversation serves as the interface to statistical methods,
  while pre-implemented algorithms perform computation---the language model
  selects tools; verified implementations produce results. This approach
  addresses concerns about correctness, data privacy, and reproducibility
  that arise when language models generate statistical code directly.
  The software contribution includes: (1)~an econometrics library in pure
  \proglang{Rust} covering regression, panel data, instrumental variables,
  difference-in-differences, discrete choice, and time series methods;
  (2)~exposure through the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for LLM integration;
  and (3)~validation against established \proglang{R} packages. Extended
  evaluation reveals practical accuracy limitations: multi-turn accuracy
  ranges from 47--60\% and parameter extraction F1 from 41--59\%, indicating
  that users should expect to correct requests in realistic workflows.
  Local deployment via Ollama enables complete data isolation.
}

%% Keywords (at least one required)
\Keywords{chat-first analytics, natural language interface, econometrics,
  \proglang{Rust}, MCP, local LLM, data privacy}
\Plainkeywords{econometrics, Rust, regression, panel data, causal inference,
  time series, MCP, R}
